title: Vector Store Schema, Versioning, Tenancy, Updates, and Deletes · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers vector store schema, versioning, tenancy, updates, and deletes into a new versioned vector-record lifecycle. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned vector-record lifecycle with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Define document, chunk, tenant, source, version, offsets, content hash, model, dimensions, vector, metadata, timestamps, and policy fields.
Reject queries when model, dimensions, preprocessing, metric, index, or schema versions are incompatible.
Make ingestion and reindexing idempotent with stable keys, generation identity, checkpoints, compare-and-swap, and reconciliation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Vector Record Schema
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 6381 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to preserve document identity, model and index versions, tenant filters, idempotency, and deletion; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

For audit the proposed evidence packet, run one convenient Rag Vector Tenancy Recovery query, omit corpus and version identity, relevance judgments, tenant policy, changed documents, abstention, citations, failure and recovery, then accept a plausible answer as RAG proof. steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Vector Tenancy Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-vector-tenancy-recovery-cu3sxr" that uses case 9961 to preserve document identity, model and index versions, tenant filters, idempotency, and deletion. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Vector Tenancy Recovery.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Vector Tenancy Recovery into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Vector Tenancy Recovery, keep Rag Vector Tenancy Recovery connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-vector-tenancy-recovery-cu3sxr
# Competency: rag-vector-tenancy-recovery.
# Case 9961: a regulated document deletion request.
# Operating constraint: keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def evidence_rag_vector_tenancy_recovery_cu3sxr(document_id: str = "policy-17", content_version: int = 6, index_version: int = 6, tenant_match: bool = True, tombstoned: bool = False, idempotent: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_cu3sxr = "cu3sxr-rxs3uc-9961"
    assert evidence_variant_cu3sxr
    evidence_axes_cu3sxr = (
        "corpuscu3sxr",
        "queryrxs3uc",
        "tenant9961",
        "indexcu3sxr9961",
        "promptrxs3uc9961",
        "evaluation9961cu3sxr",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_cu3sxr)) == 6
    if not document_id or content_version < 1:
        return False, content_version, "document-identity"
    if index_version != content_version:
        return False, index_version, "stale-index"
    if not tenant_match:
        return False, index_version, "tenant-filter"
    if tombstoned:
        return False, index_version, "deleted-document"
    if not idempotent:
        return False, index_version, "upsert-replay"
    assert index_version == content_version
    return True, index_version, "live-vector-record"
